tasks.dependencies: Routes through the LLM gateway (already the choke point)
tasks.acceptance: Ingestion wall-time for N observations is sub-linear in LLM round-trips; embedding cache hits persist across invocations (Redis); per-run stays well under the 40s budget.
tasks.risk: Low-Medium — batching importance changes prompt shape; verify scores stay sane.

tasks.title: Consolidate the 4+ OAuth flows to one canonical path per platform
tasks.description: Pick the PKCE-enabled entertainment-connectors flow as canonical for connect/callback/disconnect, route the frontend and WhatsApp connect-links there exclusively, and delete or hard-redirect the others. Centralize scopes in one config consumed by connect, refresh, and storage so persisted scopes always reflect what was granted (fixes connectors.js:693 misrepresentation).
tasks.dependencies: Disconnect-revoke helper (M1) so the consolidation absorbs it
tasks.acceptance: Each platform has exactly one reachable connect/callback/disconnect; reconnecting via any UI surface requests the same scope set; DB scopes match the actual grant.
tasks.risk: High — touches the auth path for all 10 platforms; test each end-to-end before deleting the old routes.

tasks.title: Unify the two context aggregators and two onboarding flows
tasks.description: Decide the canonical context assembler (userContextAggregator vs twinContextBuilder, ~1.6k LOC each) and the canonical onboarding flow (OnboardingFlow vs NewDiscoverFlow); extract shared retrieval/formatting and connect/interview logic into composable modules, then deprecate the other. This is where the stuck-spinner and missing-instant-wow bugs live.
tasks.acceptance: One canonical context path feeds both chat surfaces; one onboarding flow is reachable for web sign-ups; the popup-verify hardening exists on the single remaining connect path.
tasks.risk: Medium — both touch the product's most critical surfaces; diff what each fetches before merging to avoid behavior loss.

tasks.title: Add the missing test coverage on auto-tracking and budget enforcement
tasks.description: Unit-test goalTrackingService.extractMetricFromPlatformData (each metric type incl. focus_time overlapping/invalid dates) and extractMetricFromMemories (each regex, METRIC_BOUNDS rejection, special-case branches) — pure functions, no DB mock. Add a gateway-level test proving complete() and stream() both refuse once the per-instance/daily ceiling is exceeded (the $375-incident kill behavior). Add a regression test for empty-content streaming (TalkToTwin silent no-response).
tasks.acceptance: New tests fail if the focus_time math, a regex, the budget kill wiring, or the empty-stream handling regresses; all green in CI.

milestones.name: Milestone 3 — Quality & polish
milestones.goal: Reduce maintenance tax and fix the misleading-but-non-exploitable issues, paced to a solo founder's time.

tasks.title: Fix the dead mobile/dashboard nav entries and post-response side-effect loss
tasks.description: Remove or repoint the dead nav affordances that land on twin chat (BottomNav 'Knowledge', DepartmentWidget 'View all'/'+N more', DashboardV2 'Your Knowledge Base', TalkToTwin 'Review in Departments'). Move the chat-turn fire-and-forget side effects (fact extraction, STDP strengthening, new-user reflection seeding) onto Inngest so they survive the Vercel freeze, or guarantee a cron/queue path for new-user seeding.
tasks.dependencies: Milestone 2 Inngest work for the side-effect move
tasks.acceptance: No nav control routes to a redirect stub; new-user reflection seeding has a guaranteed path independent of the chat turn.

tasks.title: Reconcile README, SECURITY.md, and CLAUDE.md with reality
tasks.description: README: port 3004 (confirmed wrong at 3001), OPENROUTER_API_KEY documented, remove ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/dead dirs/dead CONTRIBUTING link. CLAUDE.md: rewrite the LLM model table to match aiModels.js (DeepSeek default, not Claude), reconcile platform roster to the 7 live, fix the chat endpoint path (/api/chat/message) and goals endpoint count. SECURITY.md: replace the stale Clerk/Anthropic/3001 content with an accurate JWT/OpenRouter/RLS trust-model summary or delete it.
tasks.acceptance: A fresh-machine run following README boots and chats successfully; the model table and platform set match code; SECURITY.md no longer describes an architecture the app doesn't have.

tasks.title: Bump axios and clean derivative advisories without major upgrades
tasks.description: npm install axios@^1.18.0 (within the existing ^1.12.2 range — zero-code lockfile bump, confirmed) to clear most of the 22 advisories and the transitive @nangohq chain; do NOT take the suggested Nango major or the bull@1.1.3 downgrade. Add an npm overrides entry for uuid if you want the bull/uuid advisory clean. Treat vite 5→8 as a separate planned upgrade, not a security hotfix.
tasks.acceptance: npm audit shows the axios + @nangohq highs cleared after dedupe; no major-version upgrades taken for security; tests green.

tasks.title: Incrementally split the god files and dedupe copy-paste
tasks.description: Split gdprImportService.js (4057 LOC) into gdpr/parsers/<platform>.js with a shared contract; collapse the duplicated extractor hierarchies to one home per platform; extract formatRelativeTime and the duplicated Message/ActionEvent/ProposalEvent types to single sources; delete confirmed dead components (SoulChatInterface cluster, AcademicHierarchy 763 LOC). Replace fetchTwinContext's three positional 16-element arrays with one keyed registry.
tasks.acceptance: No file over ~800 LOC in the touched set; shared types/utils have one definition; dead components removed and build still green; fetchTwinContext uses a keyed registry (no index-alignment invariant).
tasks.risk: Medium — the context registry refactor touches the chat hot path; verify twin context output is byte-identical before/after.

tasks.title: Add llm_usage_log retention and a bridge health alert
tasks.description: Add a weekly cleanup cron deleting llm_usage_log rows older than the dashboard window (~90d) matching the existing *-cleanup pattern. Add an external uptime/health alert on the Fly bridge /health endpoint so a single-machine outage pages the operator instead of being discovered by users; document the post-deploy 'flyctl scale count 1' step as a script.
tasks.acceptance: llm_usage_log stops growing unbounded; a bridge outage triggers an alert; the deploy step is scripted, not tribal knowledge.

quickWins.title: Enable RLS on reminders, twin_calls, and whatsmeow_* tables (copy the established service_role-only policy pattern from 20260606_create_platform_exports.sql)
quickWins.impact: Closes a confirmed per-user PII leak (reminder bodies, dialed phone numbers, call transcripts, WhatsApp E2E session keys) readable via the anon key shipped to every browser

quickWins.title: Add a gitleaks pre-commit hook + CI step
quickWins.impact: Prevents a repeat of the 6-file secret leak that put the service_role key and JWT_SECRET in history

quickWins.title: Fix README to port 3004 + OPENROUTER_API_KEY (or point it at .env.example as the single source of truth)
quickWins.impact: Unblocks first-hour onboarding — currently a new dev (or the founder on a fresh machine) gets every API call refused and every LLM path silently failing

quickWins.title: Bump axios to ^1.18.0 (within existing range, zero code changes)
quickWins.impact: Clears most of the 22 axios advisories plus the transitive @nangohq highs via dedupe with no migration risk

quickWins.title: Add a CI grep that freezes the direct-.from()-in-routes count (120 today) and the lint/tsc error baseline
quickWins.impact: Stops the layering and type/lint backlog from compounding without requiring any cleanup, converting every new violation into a hard gate

quickWins.title: Add a 'Try again' button to the DeepInterview first-question error state
quickWins.impact: Removes an onboarding dead-end where a transient failure strands the user with a non-actionable 'hit send again' message when there is nothing to send

quickWins.title: Rewrite the CLAUDE.md LLM model table to match aiModels.js (DeepSeek default, not Claude Sonnet)
quickWins.impact: Fixes a documented wrong mental model for the single biggest cost lever in a repo with a $375 cost-incident history

notFixing.item: Forcing a semver-major Nango upgrade (0.69→0.70) or downgrading bull to 1.1.3 to clear audit 'high' entries
notFixing.why: Both are derivative of the axios advisory; bumping axios to 1.18.0 clears them via dedupe. The major Nango jump risks breaking OAuth for all 10 platforms and the bull downgrade would gut the queue library — high risk for zero security gain beyond the axios fix.

notFixing.item: vite 5→8 / esbuild major upgrade for the dev-tooling advisories
notFixing.why: Real exposure is limited to a developer running npm run dev while visiting a malicious site in the same browser — not production-facing. The fix is a major requiring SWC-plugin and manual-chunk validation; schedule it as planned build-tooling work, don't let dev-only advisories drive a rushed major.

notFixing.item: Flipping strict:true / strictNullChecks on the full 79k-LOC frontend in one pass
notFixing.why: A big-bang strict migration on a codebase with strictNullChecks=false and 162 anys would generate hundreds of errors and stall feature work. The baseline-ratchet (freeze counts, fix per-directory) gets the regression protection without the cliff; full strictness is a long-tail effort, not a milestone gate.

notFixing.item: git history scrubbing (filter-repo/BFG + force-push) for the leaked secrets
notFixing.why: Rotation is the actual security control and is mandatory; history rewriting is optional cleanup that breaks every clone/fork and CI cache. For a solo-founder pre-revenue repo, rotate-and-move-on is the right cost/benefit — only scrub if the repo is about to be made public or shared.

notFixing.item: Extracting the 1.8k-2k LOC route files (soul-extraction.js, auth-simple.js) to thin controllers right now
notFixing.why: Real maintainability tax, but not exploitable and not blocking growth. The twin-chat decomposition is the proven model to follow opportunistically; spending an XL refactor here ahead of the security and scalability fixes would be mis-prioritized for this stage.

notFixing.item: Building DB-level pgTAP/SQL tests for the three-factor retrieval scoring RPC
notFixing.why: Genuinely the highest-leverage untested function, but standing up a disposable Postgres+pgvector CI job is heavy for a solo founder. The cheaper path (extract the scoring math to a pure JS function and unit-test it, as mmrRerank already is) captures most of the value; reserve the SQL-container harness for when the team grows.
